disease.name: Mitral Stenosis
disease.note: Narrowing of the mitral valve orifice, most commonly post-rheumatic. Impairs left atrial emptying, leading to pulmonary congestion.

severityAssessment.severityLabel: If pulmonary oedema/PH/haemoptysis/rapid AF -> SEVERE; else -> MILD/MODERATE

subtypes.name: Mild Mitral Stenosis (MVA >1.5 cm2)
subtypes.management: Medical: rate control for AF, diuretics PRN, anticoagulation for AF. No intervention needed.

subtypes.name: Moderate Mitral Stenosis (MVA 1.0-1.5 cm2)
subtypes.management: Medical as above. Consider percutaneous balloon mitral valvuloplasty if favorable valve morphology.

subtypes.name: Severe Mitral Stenosis (MVA <1.0 cm2)
subtypes.management: Percutaneous balloon mitral valvuloplasty (if suitable). Surgical mitral valve replacement if not suitable. Anticoagulation for AF.

laboratory.name: ASOT/anti-DNase B
laboratory.limitations: May be negative years after initial RF

imaging.name: Echocardiography
imaging.indication: MVA, mean gradient, Wilkins score, pulmonary pressure

management.protocolReference: ESC/EACTS Guidelines for Valvular Heart Disease

pathways.criteria: MVA >1.0 cm2, NYHA I-II, no pulmonary hypertension
pathways.treatment: Medical: diuretics for congestion. Beta-blocker/CCB for rate control if AF. Anticoagulation if AF. Endocarditis prophylaxis if prosthetic valve.

pathways.criteria: MVA <1.0 cm2, NYHA III-IV, suitable valve morphology (Wilkins score ≤8)
pathways.treatment: Percutaneous balloon mitral valvuloplasty. TEE before procedure to exclude LA thrombus.

pathways.criteria: MVA <1.0 cm2, NYHA III-IV, Wilkins score >8, contraindication to valvuloplasty
pathways.treatment: Mitral valve replacement (mechanical if AF or pre-existing anticoagulation, bioprosthetic if not). Post-op anticoagulation.
